title: Configuration
subtitle: View current system configuration (read-only). Use the Providers, Models, and Keys pages to make changes.

failover.title: Failover Settings
failover.enableDesc: When enabled, failed requests are automatically retried on the next available provider.
failover.statusCodesDesc: HTTP status codes that trigger a retry on the next provider. Enter comma-separated values (100–599). Defaults to all non-2xx codes except 413 and 422 when empty.
failover.errorsDesc: Network error codes that trigger a retry on the next provider. Enter comma-separated values. Defaults to ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT, ENOTFOUND when empty.

cooldown.title: Cooldown Settings
cooldown.exponentialBackoffDesc: When a provider fails, it is placed on cooldown using exponential backoff: <1>C(n) = min(C_max, C₀ × 2ⁿ)</1> where n is the consecutive failure count.
cooldown.initialDesc: C₀ — the cooldown duration after the first failure. Subsequent failures double the duration until the maximum is reached. Fractional values are supported (e.g. 0.1 = 6 seconds).
cooldown.maxDesc: C_max — the upper limit for any cooldown duration, regardless of how many consecutive failures have occurred. Fractional values are supported (e.g. 0.1 = 6 seconds).

timeout.title: Timeout Settings
timeout.heading: Upstream Request Timeout
timeout.headingDesc: The maximum time Plexus waits for an upstream provider to respond before aborting the request. Can be overridden per-provider in the provider's advanced settings.
timeout.defaultDesc: The global default for all upstream requests. Must be between 1 and 3600 seconds. When a provider-specific timeout is set, it overrides this value.

stall.title: Stall Detection
stall.heading: Stream Stall Detection
stall.headingDesc: Detect when an upstream provider is taking too long to start responding (TTFB stall) or is producing data too slowly (throughput stall). When a stall is detected, the request is aborted so the client can retry with a different provider. Leave TTFB seconds and min bytes/sec empty to disable each dimension independently.
stall.ttfbSecondsDesc: Time allowed for the provider to start producing meaningful output. Leave empty to disable TTFB stall detection. Must be between 5 and 120 seconds.
stall.ttfbBytesDesc: Byte threshold that confirms the provider is actually producing content. 100 bytes is roughly half a token. Must be between 50 and 10,000.
stall.minBpsDesc: Throughput floor after the grace period. Sliding window check. Leave empty to disable throughput stall detection. 500 B/s is approximately 2-3 tokens/sec. Must be between 50 and 5,000.
stall.windowSecondsDesc: Width of the sliding window for throughput calculation. Longer = more stable, shorter = more responsive. Must be between 3 and 30 seconds.
stall.graceSecondsDesc: Time after TTFB threshold is met before throughput enforcement begins. Allows for natural thinking/reasoning pauses. Must be between 0 and 120 seconds.

toast.loadFailed: Failed to load stall detection settings
toast.saveFailed: Failed to save stall detection settings

exploration.title: Exploration Settings
exploration.heading: Provider Exploration
exploration.headingDesc: Exploration keeps performance, latency, and end-to-end TPS data fresh across targets. By default, inline exploration occasionally diverts a small fraction of live requests to non-optimal providers. Enable background exploration to suppress inline exploration and instead fire representative probe requests in the background — live traffic is never redirected. Both modes apply to the performance, latency, and e2e_performance selectors.
exploration.backgroundDesc: When enabled, inline exploration is suppressed and Plexus fires small representative probe requests in the background. Probes appear in usage records with apiKey="probe" and attribution="background".
exploration.stalenessDesc: A target is re-probed only after this many seconds have elapsed since its last probe. Default: 600 (10 minutes). Minimum: 1.
exploration.concurrencyDesc: Maximum number of background probes Plexus runs in parallel. Per-target probes are deduplicated separately. Default: 2. Range: 1–16.
exploration.performanceRateDesc: The probability of exploring a non-optimal provider when using the performance selector. Default: 0.05 (5%).
exploration.latencyRateDesc: The probability of exploring a non-optimal provider when using the latency selector. Defaults to the Performance Exploration Rate if not explicitly set.
exploration.e2eRateDesc: The probability of exploring any provider when using the e2e_performance selector. Unlike the performance selector, exploration includes all candidates (including the current best) to keep end-to-end metrics fresh. Defaults to the Performance Exploration Rate if not explicitly set.

toast.ratesLoadFailed: Failed to load exploration rate settings
toast.backgroundLoadFailed: Failed to load background exploration settings

backup.title: Backup & Restore
backup.intro: Back up your database or restore from a previously exported backup file.
backup.sensitiveBody: This includes API keys and OAuth tokens in plaintext. Store backup files securely.
backup.configBackupDesc: Providers, models, keys, quotas, and settings only. Fast and small.
backup.fullBackupDesc: Config plus all usage logs, debug data, and errors. May take a moment for large databases.

restorePrompt.title: Restore Database?
restorePrompt.message: This will **replace all existing data** with the contents of the backup file. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?

cardLayout.title: Card Layout
cardLayout.intro: Import or export your Live Metrics card layout configuration.

toast.invalidIds: Invalid card layout: contains unknown card IDs
toast.invalidJson: Failed to import: Invalid JSON file

restart.title: Restart Plexus?
restart.message: This will briefly interrupt all ongoing requests. Are you sure you want to continue?
